title: Writing - Part 2
description: Choose one of the following writing tasks and write your answer in 140-190 words.

tasks.title: Email
tasks.prompt:
Your friend Jordan has recently started shopping online for the first time and has asked for your advice. Write an email explaining the advantages and disadvantages of online shopping, giving tips for shopping safely online, and saying whether you personally prefer online or in-store shopping.

Write your email to Jordan.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Hi Jordan,

Welcome to the world of online shopping! It can be fantastic once you know how it works, so let me share a few thoughts.

The main advantage is convenience — you can browse and buy at any hour without leaving your sofa. Prices online are often lower too, and you can easily compare products across different websites. The downside is that you cannot try things on or check quality before buying. Returns can also be a hassle.

In terms of safety, there are a few golden rules. Always shop on websites with 'https' in the address bar and a padlock symbol. Stick to well-known retailers where possible, and never save your payment details on a website you don't fully trust. Be careful of deals that seem too good to be true — they usually are.

Personally, I prefer a mix of both. I buy everyday items online but always go to a shop for clothes or anything expensive, because I like to see the quality for myself.

Hope that helps!
Best,
Nadia

tasks.title: Article
tasks.prompt:
You see this notice in an English-language consumer magazine:

'Has online shopping changed the way we think about buying things? Has it made our lives better or worse? We want your opinions — send us your article!'

Write your article.
tasks.modelAnswer:
The Online Shopping Revolution: Convenient or Dangerous?

In less than a generation, online shopping has completely transformed consumer habits. Today, almost anything — from groceries to furniture — can be ordered with a few taps on a smartphone. But has this transformation truly improved our lives?

There is no doubt that the convenience is extraordinary. Busy parents, elderly people, and anyone living in remote areas can now access products that were previously out of reach. Competition between online retailers has also driven prices down, making many goods more affordable.

However, the rise of online shopping has a darker side. Thousands of high street shops have closed as a direct result, leaving town centres empty and many people unemployed. Impulsive buying has also become a significant problem — the ease of one-click purchasing means many people spend more than they intend.

Ultimately, online shopping is a powerful tool that, used wisely, can genuinely improve daily life. However, we must be conscious of its impact on local businesses and our own spending habits. A balance is essential — and possible.

tasks.title: Letter
tasks.prompt:
You ordered a laptop from an online shop three weeks ago, but it still has not arrived. You have sent two emails to customer services with no response. Write a letter to the manager of the company explaining the situation, expressing your frustration, and demanding a solution.

Write your letter.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Dear Manager,

I am writing to formally complain about an unacceptable experience with your online store. I placed an order for a laptop (Order Reference: ONL-44872) on the 1st of this month, but after three weeks, the item has still not been delivered.

I have already contacted your customer service team twice via email — on the 10th and 17th — but I have received no response whatsoever. This silence is, frankly, unacceptable for a company that promotes itself as a reliable retailer.

The situation has caused me considerable inconvenience, as I purchased the laptop specifically for use in a university project with a strict deadline. Every day without it creates further difficulties.

I am demanding an immediate response to this letter, along with either a guaranteed delivery date or a full refund within five working days. If I do not receive a satisfactory reply, I will have no choice but to report this matter to the relevant consumer protection authorities.

I expect this to be resolved promptly.

Yours faithfully,
Kai Robinson
